description_long: This apartment building complex features two interconnected high-rise towers, with the taller structure rising prominently above the surrounding landscape. The exterior is clad in dark brown and gray materials, with a regular grid of windows running vertically along each facade, providing a sense of symmetry and order. The base of the building includes landscaped areas, paved walkways, and what appears to be a main entrance plaza. The shorter tower is attached to the side of the taller one, creating a stepped profile and offering additional rooftop green space. The windows are evenly spaced and uniformly sized, contributing to a modern, functional aesthetic. The overall design suggests a contemporary urban residential development, likely offering amenities such as lobbies, balconies, and communal outdoor areas.
description: A modern, dual-tower apartment building with a dark facade, regular window grids, and landscaped base areas, featuring a taller and a shorter interconnected structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the taller tower in full, with the shorter tower set behind and to the left, and landscaped entrance areas visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the broad face of the taller tower, with the shorter tower mostly obscured behind, and the main entrance plaza visible at the bottom.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the narrow side of the taller tower and the full width of the shorter tower, with rooftop green space and circular driveway visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the shorter tower in front and the taller tower behind, with additional landscaping and secondary entrance features at the base.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly regular, vertical and horizontal grid pattern, evenly spaced across all facades, creating a uniform and symmetrical appearance.
